title: Lily and the Missing Zebra Stripes
synopsis: When Lily discovers that all the zebras in the savanna have lost their stripes overnight, she embarks on a detective adventure to solve the mystery and help her animal friends.

pages.text: Lily loved visiting her grandmother's research station in the African savanna. Every morning, she would wake up early to watch the animals start their day. The zebras were her favorite - she loved counting their beautiful black and white stripes.

illustration.description: A young girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes stands on a wooden porch of a research station. Golden grass stretches across the savanna with zebras grazing in the distance. The scene is painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes and warm orange sunrise colors.
illustration.alt: Lily watching zebras from a research station porch at sunrise

pages.text: But this morning was different. Very different indeed. As Lily looked through her binoculars, she gasped. All the zebras looked plain white! Their beautiful black stripes had completely disappeared overnight.

illustration.description: The same girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes holds binoculars with a shocked expression. In the golden grass field, several white horses that should be zebras graze peacefully. The gouache painting uses bold confident strokes with textured painterly technique.
illustration.alt: Lily looking through binoculars at white zebras with no stripes

pages.text: "Grandma!" Lily called, running inside. "Something terrible has happened to the zebras! They've lost all their stripes!" Her grandmother looked up from her research notes, surprised. "That's impossible, dear," she said. But when she looked outside, her eyes grew wide with wonder.

illustration.description: Inside the research station, a girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes talks excitedly to her grandmother. Through the window, stripeless white zebras are visible in the warm savanna landscape. The gouache painting features thick brushstrokes and expressive characters.
illustration.alt: Lily telling her grandmother about the missing stripes

pages.text: Lily decided to become a detective. She put on her special detective hat and grabbed her notebook. "I will solve this mystery!" she declared. First, she needed to talk to the other animals to see if they had noticed anything strange.

illustration.description: A determined girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes wears a detective hat and holds a notebook. She stands confidently in the golden grass savanna with an orange sunset background. The gouache painting uses warm colors and bold strokes.
illustration.alt: Lily dressed as a detective ready to solve the mystery

pages.text: The wise old elephant named Kesi was the first animal Lily visited. "Did you see anything unusual last night?" Lily asked. Kesi flapped her big ears thoughtfully. "I did hear some giggling and rustling in the bushes near the watering hole," she trumpeted softly.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes talks to a large, expressive elephant painted with thick gouache brushstrokes. The scene shows textured painterly technique with golden grass and warm savanna colors surrounding them.
illustration.alt: Lily interviewing the wise elephant Kesi

pages.text: Next, Lily found Jengo the giraffe stretching his long neck to reach the highest leaves. "Jengo, from up there, did you see who might have taken the zebras' stripes?" The friendly giraffe looked down and said, "I saw some small, mischievous creatures heading toward the baobab tree with something black and white."

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes looks up at a tall giraffe with an expressive face. The giraffe's neck stretches toward acacia trees in the golden savanna. The gouache painting features bold confident strokes and warm orange sunset tones.
illustration.alt: Lily talking to Jengo the giraffe about the missing stripes

pages.text: Armed with these important clues, Lily headed toward the giant baobab tree. As she got closer, she could hear soft chittering and playful squeaking sounds coming from inside the hollow trunk of the ancient tree.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes approaches a massive baobab tree painted with thick gouache brushstrokes. The textured painterly technique shows golden grass around the tree with warm savanna colors and expressive shadows.
illustration.alt: Lily approaching the mysterious baobab tree

pages.text: Lily quietly peeked inside the hollow tree. What she saw made her gasp and then giggle. A family of playful monkeys was having the most wonderful time! They had painted black stripes all over themselves and were dancing around like they were zebras!

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes peeks into a tree hollow where several expressive monkeys with painted black stripes dance playfully. The scene is painted in gouache with bold confident strokes and warm colors, showing the monkeys' joyful expressions.
illustration.alt: Lily discovering monkeys painted with zebra stripes dancing inside the tree

pages.text: The smallest monkey noticed Lily watching and stopped dancing. "Oh no!" he chattered nervously. "We didn't mean to cause trouble! We just wanted to look as beautiful as the zebras. We used special mud paint that washes off easily. We were going to put the stripes back tomorrow, we promise!"

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes smiles gently at a small, expressive monkey with painted stripes who looks worried. Other painted monkeys gather around in the golden grass savanna setting, painted with thick gouache brushstrokes and textured painterly technique.
illustration.alt: Lily talking to the guilty but remorseful monkey

pages.text: Lily wasn't angry at all. Instead, she had a wonderful idea. "Why don't we all work together to paint the zebras' stripes back right now? And then maybe the zebras would like to meet you and be friends!" The monkeys cheered with excitement.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes gestures enthusiastically while surrounded by expressive monkeys with painted stripes. The gouache painting uses warm savanna colors and bold confident strokes to show everyone's happy excitement.
illustration.alt: Lily suggesting they work together to fix the problem

pages.text: Working together, Lily and the monkey family carefully painted beautiful black stripes back onto each zebra. The zebras were so happy to have their stripes again! They were also delighted to make new friends with the artistic monkeys.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes helps paint stripes on zebras alongside several expressive monkeys. The scene shows golden grass and orange sunset colors, painted with thick gouache brushstrokes and textured painterly technique.
illustration.alt: Everyone working together to restore the zebras' stripes

pages.text: From that day forward, the monkeys and zebras became the best of friends. Sometimes they would paint colorful patterns on each other just for fun. Lily had solved her first mystery and helped create new friendships in the savanna. "Being a detective is wonderful," she said, smiling as she watched her animal friends play together in the golden sunset.

illustration.description: A girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes sits contentedly watching zebras and monkeys playing together in the golden grass. The orange sunset creates a warm glow over the scene, painted with gouache using bold confident strokes and expressive animals.
illustration.alt: Lily watching her animal friends playing together at sunset

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was missing from the zebras when Lily first saw them?
questions.explanation: The zebras had lost all their beautiful black stripes overnight, which is what made Lily so concerned.

questions.question: Which animal told Lily about hearing giggling near the watering hole?
questions.explanation: Kesi the wise old elephant was the first animal Lily interviewed, and she mentioned hearing giggling and rustling.

questions.question: Where did the monkeys hide with the zebra stripes?
questions.explanation: The monkeys were hiding inside the hollow trunk of the giant baobab tree when Lily found them.

questions.question: Why did the monkeys take the zebra stripes?
choices: they wanted to look beautiful like zebras
questions.explanation: The smallest monkey explained they just wanted to look as beautiful as the zebras - they weren't trying to be mean.

questions.question: How did the story end?
choices: the monkeys and zebras became friends
questions.explanation: After working together to restore the stripes, the monkeys and zebras became best friends and continued playing together.
